name: Molasses Barbecued Ribs
recipeInstructions:
Sauce:
2 tablespoons vegetable oil
1 cup chopped onions
3/4 cup chopped green pepper
3/4 cup minced celery, including celery leaves
1 tablespoon chopped garlic
2 cups chopped drained canned tomatoes
1/3 cup red wine vinegar
1/4 cup unsulfured molasses
2 lemon wedges (each 1/8 of the whole lemon)
1/2 teaspoon ground cloves
1/2 teaspoon ground allspice
2 tablespoons tomato paste
1 tablespoon dry mustard
1 teaspoon salt
Combine the soaking mixture ingredients and soak the ribs for 2 to 4 hours.
Heat the oil in a heavy 10-inch skillet, and over low heat sauté the onions,
green pepper, celery, and garlic. Cook until the vegetables are soft, about
10 minutes.
Add the remaining ingredients and bring to a boil. Lower the heat and simmer
for 30 minutes. The sauce should be thick. Discard the lemon wedges and put
the sauce quickly through a food processor fitted with the steel blade.
Don't puree the sauce; it should retain a good deal of texture. A good
alternative to a food processor is a food mill.
Grill the ribs, basting and turning every 15-20 minutes. Serve the remaining
sauce, heated, with the ribs.
